steps.title: Recognize when linear equations have infinitely many solutions
steps.content: For a linear equation to have infinitely many solutions, both sides must be identical. This means the coefficients of $x$ must be equal and the constant terms must be equal.

steps.title: Match constant terms on both sides
steps.content:
In the equation $4x + 28 = 4x + k$, the $x$ coefficients are already equal (both are $4$). For the two sides to be identical, the constants must also be equal:
$$28 = k$$
Therefore, $k = 28$.
